zh:
希腊神话是爱琴海世界口耳相传的神与英雄故事的总汇，约公元前8世纪由荷马史诗《伊利亚特》《奥德赛》与赫西俄德《神谱》首次系统写定。《神谱》梳理了从混沌初开、乌拉诺斯与盖亚、克洛诺斯吞子到宙斯登位的神界谱系；荷马则把奥林波斯诸神写进特洛伊战场上的人间恩怨。

这套体系以奥林波斯山上的十二主神为核心，向上追溯到提坦与原初神，向下衍生出宁芙、半人马与形形色色的怪物，又通过赫拉克勒斯、珀耳修斯、忒修斯等半神英雄与人间的王族谱系相接。诸神有鲜明的性格与欲望：会嫉妒、争吵、报复，也同样受命运约束。神话与城邦生活密不可分——德尔斐的神谕、奥林匹亚的赛会、雅典卫城的神庙，都是故事落在土地上的坐标。

公元前5世纪的雅典悲剧把这些故事改写成舞台上的伦理追问，奥维德《变形记》又将其传入罗马与中世纪欧洲。文艺复兴以来，希腊神话始终是西方绘画、雕塑与文学最常取用的题材库，星座与大量科学术语至今沿用诸神与英雄之名，行星则多以这些神的罗马名字命名。
en:
Greek mythology gathers the stories of gods and heroes told across the Aegean world, first set down around the 8th century BCE in Homer's Iliad and Odyssey and Hesiod's Theogony. Hesiod traces the divine genealogy from primal Chaos through Uranus and Gaia, Cronus and his swallowed children, to the kingship of Zeus; Homer sets the Olympian gods quarreling and intervening over the battlefield of Troy.

At the center stand the twelve gods of Mount Olympus, with the Titans and primordial powers behind them, nymphs, centaurs, and a menagerie of monsters below, and demigod heroes such as Heracles, Perseus, and Theseus binding the divine family to the royal houses of Greece. The gods have vivid tempers and appetites — they envy, quarrel, and take revenge — yet even they bow to fate. Myth was woven into civic life: the oracle at Delphi, the games at Olympia, and the temples of the Athenian acropolis all fixed the stories to particular ground.

Athenian tragedy of the 5th century BCE reworked the myths into moral drama on stage, and Ovid's Metamorphoses carried them into Rome and medieval Europe. Since the Renaissance they have remained the most heavily drawn-upon store of subjects in Western painting, sculpture, and literature, and the names of the gods and heroes persist in the constellations and in scientific vocabulary, while the planets carry the same gods' names, mostly in their Roman forms.
